metrics.narrative: The business problem is clear and articulated by the buyer but never quantified. Felicia Kakora described a 'web based system that... has come to the end of its life cycle' where 'a lot of what we currently are doing is very manually labored' (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026). The pain is fragmented requests, high manual effort, and limited visibility into compliance maintenance. No cost, headcount, cycle-time, or savings figure was stated on the call, and Customer_Business_Problem__c / Business_Objectives__c capture the same qualitative pain without numbers. Quantifying the manual-labour cost and compliance-risk exposure is the move to build a defensible value case.

champion.narrative: Felicia Kakora is a warm, engaged champion candidate but not yet a true champion. She self-initiated the meeting after seeing Zycus at the Smart Procurement event, was openly positive ('it's already looking promising, I must say') and signalled intent to widen the conversation: she wants to 'come back if it's not just for master data management... from a procurement and supply chain management perspective' (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026). X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c lists her as 'business evaluator/champion candidate'. What is developing: genuine interest and a stated willingness to open doors. What is still missing: she explicitly has no buying authority and has not yet introduced the procurement/supply-chain custodian, so advocacy to power is unproven.

competition.narrative: The named competitor field (Competitors__c) is blank, but the real competition is reconstructed from the call: the primary threat is the internal in-flight digital-procurement solution Eskom is already pursuing ('that's the solution that they're currently anyway looking at') plus do-nothing inertia, since an enterprise tender may already be closed. SAP is the entrenched ERP incumbent (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c: 'SAP ERP with an existing legacy web-based supplier management system'), which is both a competitive anchor and an integration requirement Zycus addresses with its native SAP connector. Zycus wins by positioning as a complementary supplier-master-data and Merlin AI layer that plugs into the existing digital solution rather than displacing it, exactly the 'bottom-up' angle Felicia invited.

sources: Avoma intro call 30 Jun 2026 (Felicia Kakora, Inno Lebere)
identify_pain.narrative: Operational pain is confirmed and owned by the master-data function. Felicia and Inno Lebere described a legacy web-based supplier system at end-of-life forcing manual, labour-intensive onboarding and ongoing compliance maintenance (COIDA, B-BBEE, tax) with limited automation for notifications and updates (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026). Inno specifically asked 'where does the maintenance happen?' confirming the post-onboarding compliance-maintenance pain is the team's daily problem. Brice also surfaced fragmented, email/flat-file-driven intake causing maverick spend and compliance risk. The pain owner is Felicia's supplier master-data team; the enterprise pain owner (broader procurement) is implied but not yet engaged.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are known. As a South African state-owned enterprise, Eskom procurement is bound by the relevant stakeholder requirements and a formal tender process, which Felicia described in detail (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026). The forcing function is that a digital-procurement tender may already be closed: 'It's a pity you didn't bid when the tender went out... but I don't think it's late.' Visibility would come from learning whether the in-flight tender will be reopened or whether a separate supplier-management procurement is viable. No MSA/SOW, legal, or InfoSec activity exists yet at this stage.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged. Felicia explicitly placed buying authority elsewhere: 'if it is that you need to engage on a bigger platform in terms of procurement and supply chain management, then that will be a whole different conversation with the custodian of procurement and supply chain management' (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026). X10a_Sponsor__c reads 'To be identified (Procurement/Supply Chain Executive)' confirming the gap is genuine, not a hygiene issue. The account roster names plausible power (Jainthree Sankar, Chief Procurement Officer; Pieter le Roux, GM Procurement & Supply Chain Strategy) but none is proven on this opp. Identifying and reaching the procurement/supply-chain custodian is the rank-1 move.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. The call sequence so far is a single introductory meeting (30 Jun 2026) attended only by the master-data team (Felicia Kakora and her report Inno Lebere) plus Zycus. Felicia flagged a structural constraint: an internal digital-procurement tender is already in flight and 'I doubt eCompliance is gonna go back and say, okay, we're gonna reopen the market' (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026), raising real questions about whether an enterprise procurement path even exists for Zycus. What_is_the_decision_process__c is blank. Mapping how Eskom would procure an enterprise S2P platform, and whether the in-flight tender can be expanded or worked around, is essential.

sources: Avoma intro call 30 Jun 2026 (Felicia Kakora, Inno Lebere, Brice)
decision_criteria.narrative: Functional criteria are emerging from the discovery call and are well captured: supplier master-data management with onboarding and ongoing maintenance, mandatory regulatory documents (COIDA/SARS, B-BBEE, tax compliance), a supplier self-service portal, native SAP ERP synchronization, configurable workflows, and supplier risk & performance scoring using external data (Dun & Bradstreet, EcoVadis) (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026; Business_Objectives__c). These are confirmed for Felicia's master-data scope. The enterprise-wide S2P evaluation criteria (the broader procurement custodian's requirements) are not yet known and What_is_the_decision_process__c is blank.

She brought her direct report Inno Lebere into the call, a positive engagement signal.
confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is fresh and positive (warm self-initiated intro call on 30 Jun 2026), not cooling. The risk is breadth of access, not interest.

north_star_verdict.math: Opportunity created 29 Jun 2026 and set to Qualified the same day; close date 2027-02-28 is 243 days out. Introductory call held 30 Jun 2026 (today). Time-in-stage is 1 day, so no slip yet. For a $150k 8-module S2P transformation a ~8-month runway is realistic IF the broader procurement custodian is engaged soon; the only EARLY-tier risk is no power mapped, not timing.
evidence: Opportunity created and moved Initial Interest -> Qualified on 29 Jun 2026; close date pushed 2026-12-29 -> 2027-02-28 same day (SF OpportunityFieldHistory).
Introductory meeting held 30 Jun 2026 with Felicia Kakora (Senior Manager: Master Data Management) and her direct report Inno Lebere (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026).
Felicia stated she 'cannot make inferences to such a solution because I'm only representing a very small section of the value chain' (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026) - confirming no buying authority yet.
north_star_verdict.headline: Fresh, live early-stage deal: strong introductory call on 30 Jun 2026 with clear pain, but engagement is single-threaded to a mid-level evaluator who cannot buy S2P.

flags: Re-establish momentum immediately: the 8-month silence between the intro call (16 Oct 2025) and opp creation (29 Jun 2026) signals low urgency or a partner-led process that stalled. Use the 30 Jun call with Felicia Kakora to quantify the pain (cost of manual sourcing, compliance risk), anchor to a compelling event (fiscal year, audit, transformation mandate), and lock a demo with multiple stakeholders within 2 weeks.

Map the economic buyer and multi-thread beyond Felicia Kakora. The only contact role is a mid-level evaluator (Senior Manager Master Data Management). On the 30 Jun call, ask Felicia who approves the budget for an S2P platform purchase of this scale, then secure an introduction to the CPO (Jainthree Sankar) or CIO (Faith Burn, Peshy Mog, Shaheen Osman) within 1 week.

Surface the competitive landscape and the decision process. The buyer conducted market research before reaching out (16 Oct 2025 call) but no competitors or evaluation timeline were named. On the 30 Jun call, ask Felicia who else Eskom is evaluating, what the market research concluded, and when Eskom aims to make a decision. If no formal process exists, propose a mutual close plan anchored to a fiscal or transformation deadline.
Demo requested 16 Oct 2025 never confirmed as delivered; no buyer feedback or evaluation criteria on record.
Partner (Impela) led intro call but no follow-up since Oct 2025; unclear if partner is actively engaged or if Zycus must own buyer relationship directly.
Rep-initiated follow-up scheduled 30 Jun (today) awaiting buyer confirmation; no verified two-way buyer touch logged in Salesforce (LastActivityDate: none).

Beat SAP incumbency with a plug-in narrative, not a rip-and-replace: SAP already won the prior EY-led S2C on relationship and runs Eskom's ERP. Lead with the native SAP connector and Merlin-in-Teams so Zycus is the best-of-breed layer that completes the in-flight solution rather than competing with it.

Win on the integration wedge, not displacement: with an internal digital-procurement solution already in flight, position Zycus as a complementary supplier-master-data + Merlin AI layer that plugs into SAP and the existing tool, exactly the bottom-up angle Felicia invited.
best_practice_check.summary: Strong, warm first call but the deal is single-threaded to a no-authority evaluator with an open question on whether an enterprise buying path even exists. Win-strategy priorities focus on power access, the integration wedge, and a value case.

competitive_position.summary: No external vendor is in an active bake-off as of 30 Jun 2026. The single strongest current threat is Eskom's own in-flight internal digital-procurement solution combined with do-nothing inertia, because an enterprise procurement tender may already be closed and may not reopen. SAP is the ERP incumbent and integration anchor, not a competing S2P bid. Zycus's path is to position as a complementary master-data + Merlin AI layer that plugs into the existing solution.

competitors.name: do_nothing
competitors.source: Inferred from 8-month gap (16 Oct 2025 intro to 29 Jun 2026 opp creation), no compelling event, no quantified pain, no urgency signal
competitors.how_we_win: Surface a quantified business case (spend visibility, supplier risk reduction, cycle-time savings) and position Merlin AI negotiation agent as a leap beyond manual/legacy procurement; create urgency by tying to a forcing event (budget cycle, contract expiration, audit finding, executive mandate).

competitors.how_we_win: Lead with the native SAP connector so SAP is an integration asset, not a competing bid; remove integration risk as a buying objection.

competitors.how_we_win: Find the master-data and supplier-maintenance gaps the in-flight solution does not address and offer Zycus as the plug-in that completes it.

competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as a complementary supplier-master-data and Merlin AI layer that plugs into the in-flight solution rather than displacing it; win the bottom-up master-data scope Felicia controls.

The agentic narrative is resonating and should be the centerpiece of the deeper demo.

No economic buyer, decision maker, or champion identified; only mid-level evaluator (Felicia Kakora) in contact roles, no power mapped

Re-establish momentum immediately: the 8-month silence between the intro call (16 Oct 2025) and opp creation (29 Jun 2026) signals low urgency or a partner-led process that stalled. Use the 30 Jun call with Felicia Kakora to quantify the pain (cost of manual sourcing, compliance risk), anchor to a compelling event (fiscal year, audit, transformation mandate), and lock a demo with multiple stakeholders within 2 weeks.

Map the economic buyer and multi-thread beyond Felicia Kakora. The only contact role is a mid-level evaluator (Senior Manager Master Data Management). On the 30 Jun call, ask Felicia who approves the budget for an S2P platform purchase of this scale, then secure an introduction to the CPO (Jainthree Sankar) or CIO (Faith Burn, Peshy Mog, Shaheen Osman) within 1 week.

Surface the competitive landscape and the decision process. The buyer conducted market research before reaching out (16 Oct 2025 call) but no competitors or evaluation timeline were named. On the 30 Jun call, ask Felicia who else Eskom is evaluating, what the market research concluded, and when Eskom aims to make a decision. If no formal process exists, propose a mutual close plan anchored to a fiscal or transformation deadline.
